ocr.text: Study Day or Week  Activity and efficacy (%)
Tick species  R. sanguineus 50 ticks (25♂ 25♀)  I. ricinus 40 ticks (20♂ 20♀)
Day 0  Collars fitted to dogs in treated group
The following table presents a comprehensive analysis of the efficacy of various activities conducted to manage tick
populations, specifically focusing on the species Rhipicephalus sanguineus and Ixodes ricinus. The study monitored these tick
species over a designated period, categorized by distinct Study Day or Week intervals. Each interval allows for a systematic

evaluation of the impact of specific interventions on tick reduction, providing valuable insights into their effectiveness. 

In the
context of this research, 50 R. sanguineus ticks (comprising 25 males and 25 females) and 40 I. ricinus ticks (20 males and 20
females) were subjected to various control measures. The Activity column outlines the specific interventions implemented
during the study, while the efficacy (%) column quantifies the percentage reduction in tick populations attributable to each
activity. This structured approach not only facilitates the comparison of different interventions but also aids in identifying the
most effective strategies for tick management. By analyzing the data, stakeholders can make informed decisions regarding the
adoption of targeted pest control measures to mitigate the risks associated with tick-borne diseases, thereby enhancing public
health outcomes.
